asr_es: Buenos días. Yo también tengo que agradecer al señor Stek este informe tan magnífico que ha realizado después del trabajo de estos últimos meses en esta comisión con toda la aportación de los expertos que han venido de los diferentes países. El trabajo sobre la Comisión Mare nos ha permitido profundizar sobre la catástrofe del Prestige y otras, sus consecuencias medioambientales y la necesidad de avanzar en la seguridad marítima. El colega Stek hace un informe excelente después de los debates con expertos y la reunión con el capitán. Queda patente que la decisión de alejar el Prestige fue un error, por lo que urge hacer una relación de puertos refugios, tal como ha dicho la comisaria anteriormente. Que ya se ha ido avanzando en las costas europeas y, como bien se dice en el informe, dotarla de compensaciones económicas para paliar los problemas medioambientales y otros que se deberían por la acogida de los buques de carga de materias peligrosas. La comisión tiene que ser más ágil y contundente al aplicar sus directivas a los Estados miembros en materia de seguridad marítima. Así como crear las condiciones. hypothesis_en: I should also like to thank Mr Stek for this excellent report which he has produced after the work of the last few months in this committee with all the input of the experts who have come from the different countries. The work on the Committee on the Seas has enabled us to delve into the Prestige disaster and others, its environmental consequences and the need to make progress on maritime safety.
reference_en: two and (3.11) a (1.34) half minutes good morning I'd also like to thank Mr Sterckx for this magnificent report (1.05) which he has written after the six months of work carried out in the Temporary Committee with all of the contributions by experts who came from the different states involved the work on the MARE committee has made it possible for us to look into greater depth in the Prestige into the Prestige disaster and other disasters looking at their environmental consequences and the need to make headway on safety at sea Mr Sterckx has given us an excellent report after the debates with experts and the meeting with the captain of the ship it's clear (1.08) that the decisions surrounding the Prestige were a mistake now w= as the Commissioner said we now need to improve the policy of safe havens in European coasts and as is said in the report quite rightly we need to work out economic compensation schemes to offset the environmental consequences and other consequences (1.39) which occur because of the reception of vessels with dangerous goods on board we have to= the Commission has to apply this directive strictly to the Member States dealing with maritime safety we also have to create the necessary conditions (1.04) cooperating at an international level (1.48) now it's a positive report (1.65) certain paragraphs are missing how= however which we feel to be fundamental for environmental security and for workers conditions at sea the GUE (1.14) (3.32) there is a principle and I I think we should list now the principles which we feel should be included in the report there is an environmental pr= principle pol= which is called polluter pays therefore Amendment three by the GUE should be included that would be to add a new paragraph whereby adopting decisions geared towards making sure that those responsible for disasters pay entirely for the costs involved ship owners freight owners the owners of the cargo and the classifying companies as well as in the interests of safety we should have a ban on flags of convenience in the territorial waters of Europe (2.16) now this was one of the issues raised in the Committee we need to support the amendment on better working conditions for workers better pay (1.52) and better assessment of their work we cannot take measures without looking at the situation of the workers themselves and we have to see whether they are exploited we cannot move forward with safety measures if we do not establish in the report exactly what the situation of seafaring workers is looking at their welfare in other words therefore in the GUE we believe that these amendments are absolutely vital in order to ensure that such a positive report be one hundred percent positive at the end of the day thank you
